title: Teyana Taylor Wins Golden Globe for 'One Battle After Another'
text: Teyana Taylor claimed her first Golden Globe award on January 11, 2026, winning best performance by a female actor in a supporting role in any motion picture for her work in Paul Thomas Anderson's One Battle After Another. The win came as a surprise to Taylor. During her acceptance speech, she revealed her initial disbelief about her chances, stating: I almost didn't write a speech because I didn't think I'd win. Taylor delivered an emotional acceptance address, joking about her children watching from upstairs and thanking director Anderson, whom she playfully referred to as Paul Let Him Cook Anderson for his vision of the film. Most significantly, Taylor used her platform to address broader representation issues, declaring: Our softness is not a liability. Our depth is not too much. Our light does not need permission to shine. We belong in every room we walk into. The film itself was a major contender at the ceremony, earning nine nominations—the most of any film that year.
